features.summary: Occidental reported record Q3 2021 free cash flow, the highest since at least 2000 despite including periods of significantly higher oil prices. The company generated strong operational performance across all segments, with OxyChem delivering its strongest quarter in over 30 years. Hurricane Ida temporarily impacted Gulf of Mexico and OxyChem operations but facilities are back online with no lasting effects. The company repaid $4.3 billion of debt in Q3, exiting with $2.1 billion unrestricted cash and approximately $14 billion total debt repaid since August 2019. Production reached the high end of guidance and the company completed its large-scale divestiture program with the $750 million Ghana asset sale.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year 2021 production guidance to 1.155 million BOE/day while maintaining $2.9 billion capital guidance. Q4 production is expected at 1.14 million BOE/day with higher capital spend due to maintenance timing and accelerated Permian rig starts. OxyChem earnings guidance was increased for the third time in 2021, with Q4 expected to exceed the record Q3 performance. The company expects to reach its $25 billion net debt target sooner than anticipated, after which it will prioritize growing the fixed dividend while maintaining a $40 WTI breakeven. Management emphasized becoming a carbon management company through direct air capture technology development.
features.risks: The company faces potential cost inflation pressures as oil prices remain elevated, though management believes established contracts and efficiency improvements will help mitigate impacts. Hurricane and weather-related disruptions remain a risk for Gulf of Mexico and chemical operations. The success of the carbon capture business depends heavily on government support through 45Q tax credits and direct pay mechanisms. Commodity price volatility could impact the sustainability of dividend increases and debt reduction plans. Supply chain constraints and permitting challenges, particularly in Colorado, could affect operational efficiency.
features.key_drivers: Strong commodity prices, particularly oil and natural gas, are driving exceptional free cash flow generation. OxyChem benefits from tight supply-demand dynamics following weather disruptions and strong construction demand, with PVC operating rates over 80% and domestic demand up 13% year-over-year. Operational efficiency improvements continue across all basins, with 16% faster drilling in Delaware Basin and multiple records set in Rockies, Gulf of Mexico, and Oman. The company's integrated chemical business model provides advantages through exposure to multiple end markets including polyurethanes, TiO2, and water treatment beyond traditional PVC markets.
